inputs.label: Annual Salary
inputs.businessContext: Base annual salary of the employee. Standard compensation practice.

inputs.label: Benefits Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of salary for benefits (health, retirement, etc.). Typical range 25-35%.

inputs.label: Overhead Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Indirect costs (facilities, equipment, admin). Industry benchmark 40-60%.

inputs.label: Profit Margin Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Desired profit margin on labor. Typical 10-30%.

inputs.label: Paid Hours Per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total paid hours (40 hrs/week * 52 weeks). Standard full-time.

inputs.label: Billable Hours Per Year
inputs.businessContext: Hours actually billed to clients. Typically 85-90% of paid hours.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy. Affects adjustment factor.

thresholds.billableHoursPerYear < 0.8 * paidHoursPe: Low utilization warning: billable hours less than 80% of paid hours.
thresholds.overheadPercent > 100: High overhead warning: overhead exceeds 100% of direct costs.

suggestedActions: If utilization low: increase billable hours or reduce paid hours.
If overhead high: review indirect costs for reduction opportunities.
If profit margin too low: consider adjusting pricing or cost structure.
